text: DAY 1 MANDATE: IF A CRITICAL SUBSYSTEM IS UNWINNABLE, YOU MUST SCRAP THE UNIT.
details: [CRITICAL STEP] Open the Port Map, click the red ERROR text to confirm the fault, then hit SCRAP.
[CODE] If the returned payload code cannot be patched to match the protocol, scrap it.
[GEAR] A cracked input or output drive is a hard fail — cannot be repaired.
[FLOW] If the first live lead from the power source is missing, scrap the machine.
[GRID] If cells are stuck in dead space (!) or there aren't enough charge dominoes for the minimum, scrap it.

text: DAY 2 MANDATE: STRICT COMPLIANCE REQUIRED. SCRAP ANY UNIT THAT BREAKS COMPLIANCE FILTERS.
details: [CODE] Outputs may drift or come back corrupted. Noncompliant returns (wrong characters, mismatched protocol) are scrap signals.
[GEAR] Rejection-marked rusty parts may appear. If one is required to connect the drives, scrap the machine.
[FLOW] Green and orange filters recolor power routing. If a required module receives the wrong power class, scrap it.
[GRID] Grids now use `<` regions. Broken glyph markers (e.g. `#`, `@`) and intentionally isolated charge cells are compliance failures. Scrap.

text: DAY 3 MANDATE: ZERO TOLERANCE FOR HAZARDOUS INSTABILITY. IF IT SPARKS OR CONTAMINATES, SCRAP IT.
details: [CODE] A green debugger bug accompanied by red sparks indicates hazardous contamination. Scrap immediately.
[GEAR] Deadlock clamps create true dead space. Red sparks mark unsafe discharge. Any live spark state is a mandatory scrap.
[FLOW] Multiple power sources enabled. RED power lines and Crackling components are severely unsafe fire hazards. Scrap the unit.
[GRID] Grids mix groups, `<`, `=`, AND `!=` links. ANY corrupted red clown/wildcard domino denotes an overridden system. Automatic scrap.

text: UNOFFICIAL: The underground Umbrella network needs your help to build rebellion units.
details: This is a story lead, not a payroll directive. Keep an ear on the comms channel.
If you receive unusual umbrella parts or purple circuits, they might be needed for specific machines.
Rebellion circuits overrule factory mandated protocols, but you must still manually clear the board.
